content.choice: Amend constitution to allow stages to be declared vacuously passed when empirical evidence demonstrates the frozen element is not a binding constraint
content.rationale: Sessions 6-11 (7 sessions) tested Stage 3 through 5 independent approaches. All converged on the same result: eta can be made adaptive (healthy distributions, non-trivial values) but produces zero measurable performance difference. The original 'do not skip stages' rule creates a deadlock when a stage is empirically vacuous — you cannot pass it (because 'beats fixed' is undefined on a flat landscape) and cannot proceed past it.
content.evidence: c011 (system insensitive to eta heterogeneity), c012 (derivative tower collapses at order 1), c013 (resp_z family exhausted), Session 8 validation (p=0.999 training, p=0.688 novel on 10 seeds), Session 11 delta_stability (+0.0068 within noise), Session 11 delta_correlation (anti-signal eliminated)
amendment_criteria: Multiple independent approaches tested with adequate statistical power
Mechanism works — frozen element can be made adaptive with non-trivial, non-degenerate behavior
No measurable performance difference between adaptive and frozen versions
Theoretical explanation exists for why element is not a binding constraint
content.consequence: Eta marked as 'adaptive (vacuous)' in frozen frame. Stage 3 vacuously passed. Project advances to Stage 4 (structural constants). Vacuous flag requires revalidation if later stages reveal conditional dependency.
alternatives: Continue searching for Stage 3 signals indefinitely
Redefine Stage 3 exit criterion to remove 'beats fixed' requirement
